violations.text: The establishment Certified Food Safety Manager License was observed to be expired. Certified Food Safety Manager License to be renewed. (Application supplied at time of inspection.) New Violation. Correct By: 05/14/2026

Working containers of dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 05/20/2026

Plastic cups/bowls are being used as scoops in containers of dry ingredients. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. New Violation. Correct By: 05/20/2026

Single-use/single-service containers observed removed from packaging were not stored inverted to protect from possible contamination. Containers were rearrange/inverted for proper storage at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/14/2026

The hot water plumbing line to the hot water control handle of the hand sink of the faucet in the cooking area was observed turned off due to a malfunctioning hot water control valve (hot water leaks by even when faucet hot water is turned off). Faucet is to be adjusted/repaired/replaced to allow for proper functioning faucet at the cooking area hand sink. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2026

The non-food contact surfaces of the following were observed to have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: - Refrigeration gaskets - Shelving below prep tables - Dry food (ingredient) storage containers - Cookline equipment surfaces Equipment is to be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean and to preclude the buildup of soil and debris New Violation. Correct By: 05/27/2026

Walls in areas of the kitchen and prep rooms were observed to have a buildup of soil and debris behind/below pieces of equipment and the 3-bay sink. Physical facilities are to be cleaned at a frequency necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 05/27/2026

The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was 100 parts per million (ppm). According to the manufacturer's use directions, the concentration of the quaternary ammonium must be 200-400 ppm. PIC added more sanitizing solution until quaternary ammonium reached 200 ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/11/2024

The following non-food contact surfaces were observed to have an accumulation of food residue, and/or other debris: 1) shelving under all prep tables 2) inside hot holding unit 3) fan covers in the walk-in cooler New Violation. Correct By: 01/11/2024
The hand sink in the front prep area was observed in poor repair (faucet leaking). New Violation. Correct By: 01/25/2024

The walls in the front prep area has an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 01/11/2024
The ceiling in the front prep area is covered with tiles that are not smooth, durable, and easily cleanable. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2024
